meta.title: Nephrology Literature Update
meta.query: Clinical nephrology — RCTs, systematic reviews & meta-analyses (humans, English)
meta.source: Generated by Google NotebookLM, grounded in PubMed (NLM) abstracts. Each entry links to its PubMed record and DOI.
meta.note: Summaries and PICO are NotebookLM analyses of the source abstracts; criteria marked “not specified” were not stated.

articles.authors: Neuen BL, Heerspink HJL, Perkovic V, Cherney DZI, Lam CSP, et al.
articles.title: Efficacy and safety of finerenone in patients with chronic kidney disease: an individual participant data pooled analysis (INFINITY).
articles.study_type: Individual participant data meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
articles.summary: Finerenone reduced the risk of the composite kidney outcome by 24% (HR 0.76 [95% CI 0.68-0.86]) and the composite cardiovascular outcome by 20% (HR 0.80 [95% CI 0.70-0.91]). The treatment also reduced the risk of all-cause death (HR 0.88 [95% CI 0.79-0.99]) with effects consistent across various disease etiologies and levels of glycaemia.

pico.population: Patients with chronic kidney disease (CKD) across varying etiologies and levels of glycaemia, eGFR, and albuminuria.
pico.outcome: Kidney failure or sustained 57% or more decline in eGFR; hospitalisation for heart failure or cardiovascular death.

inclusion: Participants from FIDELIO-DKD, FIGARO-DKD, and FIND-CKD trials

appraisal.bottom_line: Finerenone reduces the risk of CKD progression (HR 0.76) and cardiovascular death or heart failure hospitalization (HR 0.80) across diverse patient populations.
appraisal.relevance: Supports finerenone as a foundational therapy for CKD regardless of diabetic status or baseline renal function.
appraisal.caveat: Hyperkalaemia occurs more frequently with finerenone, though absolute rates of hospitalisation for it remain low.

articles.authors: Heerspink HJL, Jardine MJ, Kohan DE, Lafayette R, Levin A, et al.
articles.title: Atrasentan in patients with IgA nephropathy (ALIGN): final 2·5-year results from a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 3 trial.
articles.summary: Atrasentan slowed kidney function decline with a total eGFR slope difference of 1.4 mL/min/1.73 m2 per year (95% CI 0.5-2.3) compared to placebo over 2.5 years. The between-group difference in eGFR change from baseline at week 132 was 2.6 mL/min per 1.73 m2 (95% CI 0.1-5.0).

pico.population: Adults with biopsy-proven IgA nephropathy, eGFR ≥30 mL/min per 1.73 m2, and proteinuria ≥1.0 g/day despite stable RAS inhibition.
pico.outcome: Change from baseline in eGFR at week 136; total eGFR slope.

inclusion: eGFR of at least 30 mL/min per 1.73 m2
Urinary protein excretion of at least 1.0 g/day
exclusion: Initiation of restricted or alternative IgA nephropathy medications or kidney replacement therapy (for secondary eGFR endpoint analysis)

appraisal.bottom_line: Atrasentan 0.75 mg daily reduces proteinuria and preserves kidney function (1.4 mL/min/1.73 m2/year slope benefit) in patients with IgA nephropathy.
appraisal.relevance: Provides high-quality evidence for endothelin A receptor antagonism as an effective long-term treatment for IgAN, including when used with SGLT2 inhibitors.
appraisal.caveat: The primary eGFR change at week 136 narrowly missed statistical significance (p=0.057).

articles.authors: Lee TC, Barina LA, Walls G, Goodman AL, Yahav D, et al.
articles.title: Cefazolin for Methicillin-Susceptible Staphylococcus aureus Bacteremia.
articles.study_type: Open-label, randomized, Bayesian adaptive platform trial
articles.summary: Cefazolin was noninferior to antistaphylococcal penicillins for 90-day mortality (15.0% vs 17.0%; aOR 0.81, 95% CrI 0.59-1.12). Cefazolin significantly reduced the risk of acute kidney injury (13.9% vs 19.6%; aOR 0.67, 95% CrI 0.50-0.89).

pico.population: Adult patients with penicillin-resistant, methicillin-susceptible S. aureus bacteremia.
pico.outcome: Death from any cause within 90 days; development of acute kidney injury within 14 days.

appraisal.bottom_line: Cefazolin is noninferior to antistaphylococcal penicillins for mortality and superior for renal safety, reducing AKI incidence by approximately 30% (aOR 0.67).
appraisal.relevance: Strongly suggests cefazolin should be the preferred agent for MSSA bacteremia to prevent treatment-related AKI.
appraisal.caveat: The trial was open-label, which could influence the detection or reporting of secondary outcomes like AKI.

articles.authors: Kandzari DE, Mahfoud F, Townsend RR, Kario K, Weber MA, et al.
articles.title: Three-Year Durability of Radiofrequency Renal Denervation: SPYRAL HTN-ON MED.
articles.summary: Radiofrequency renal denervation (RDN) showed significant and durable reductions in 24-hour ambulatory systolic BP compared to sham at 36 months (treatment difference -4.7 mm Hg; P=0.0028). Office systolic BP also remained significantly lower (difference -7.5 mm Hg; P=0.0002) with a similar medication burden between groups.

pico.population: Patients with uncontrolled hypertension on 1 to 3 standard antihypertensive medications.
pico.outcome: 24-hour ambulatory systolic blood pressure; office systolic blood pressure; medication burden; safety.

appraisal.bottom_line: Radiofrequency renal denervation provides durable blood pressure lowering effects (ambulatory SBP difference -4.7 mm Hg) through 3 years of follow-up.
appraisal.relevance: Confirms the long-term efficacy and safety of catheter-based RDN for patients failing standard medical therapy.
appraisal.caveat: A high crossover rate (74%) in the sham group required imputation of blood pressure values, which may affect the precision of the long-term treatment effect estimate.

articles.authors: Møller M, Andersen TH, Borg R, Christensen R, Bressendorff I, et al.
articles.title: Complication rates of 16- and 18-gauge needles for native kidney biopsies: a systematic review and proportional meta-analysis.
articles.summary: The pooled major complication rate was 1.83% (95% CI: 1.20-2.79) for 16-gauge needles and 1.29% (95% CI: 0.78-2.13) for 18-gauge needles, showing no statistically significant difference. Mean glomerular yield was similar between 16G (18.8) and 18G (17.5) needles.

pico.population: Adults undergoing real-time ultrasound-guided native kidney biopsies.
pico.outcome: Major complication rate; mean glomerular yield.

inclusion: Randomized trials and cohort studies of real-time ultrasound-guided biopsies

appraisal.bottom_line: No significant difference exists in major complication rates or diagnostic yield between 16G and 18G needles for native kidney biopsies.
appraisal.relevance: Supports the safety of both needle sizes; selection can be based on physician preference or specific patient factors.
appraisal.caveat: Results are limited by the use of single-arm cohorts and study-level indirect comparisons with high heterogeneity.

articles.authors: Yun HJ, Kim DH, Chung MY, Min JY.
articles.title: Opioid-Sparing Effects of Peripheral Nerve Blocks in Kidney Transplant Recipients: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.summary: Peripheral nerve blocks (PNBs) significantly reduced 24-h morphine consumption by -16.20 mg (95% CI -24.66 to -7.74; P=0.0002) compared to control analgesia. No studies reported an increase in adverse events associated with the use of PNBs.

appraisal.bottom_line: Peripheral nerve blocks effectively reduce 24-hour postoperative morphine requirements by roughly 16 mg in kidney transplant recipients.
appraisal.relevance: PNBs should be considered as part of a multimodal analgesic strategy to minimize opioid-related risks in transplant patients.
appraisal.caveat: High heterogeneity (I²=99%) among studies necessitates caution in interpreting the exact magnitude of the sparing effect across different block techniques.

articles.authors: Liu M, Wu Y, Ye F, Liu W, Deng X, et al.
articles.title: Risk prediction models for malnutrition in dialysis patients in China: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.summary: The pooled prevalence of malnutrition among dialysis patients was 41%. Eight significant predictors were identified: age, serum calcium, Kt/V, triglycerides, sex, vitamin D, NT-proBNP, and comorbid diabetes.

pico.intervention: Use of risk prediction models for malnutrition.
pico.outcome: Prevalence of malnutrition; predictor significance; model discriminatory performance.

appraisal.bottom_line: Malnutrition affects 41% of dialysis patients, and validated models (pooled AUC 0.83) can help identify those at risk using clinical and laboratory markers.
appraisal.relevance: Emphasizes the critical need for routine nutritional screening in dialysis units.
appraisal.caveat: All evaluated models were at high risk of bias due to methodological limitations and poor reporting of data analysis.

articles.authors: Xie CM, Shen MQ, Yao YT, Yang K, He LX, et al.
articles.title: Hypotension prediction index in surgical patients: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
articles.study_type: Systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
articles.summary: HPI-guided management significantly reduced the incidence and duration of intraoperative hypotension (WMD -10.61 min, P < .00001). However, this improvement did not result in a significant reduction in postoperative acute kidney injury (OR 0.83, 95% CI 0.64-1.07, P=.16).

pico.outcome: Incidence and duration of intraoperative hypotension; postoperative acute kidney injury.

appraisal.bottom_line: While the Hypotension Prediction Index effectively reduces intraoperative hypotensive time, it has not yet been proven to reduce the risk of postoperative AKI (OR 0.83).
appraisal.relevance: Clinicians should be aware that physiological hemodynamic improvements from machine learning may not immediately translate into hard clinical kidney benefits.
appraisal.caveat: The meta-analysis for AKI may be underpowered as it was a secondary outcome in many included trials.
